skill.name: converting-email-templates
skill.description: Convert email templates from SendGrid, Mailgun, Mandrill, Postmark, Brevo, and Amazon SES to Mailtrap-compatible Handlebars syntax with reports for manual review.
skill.summary: Converts provider email templates to Mailtrap Handlebars format and flags manual migration work.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the critical static heuristic: the high-volume backtick, C2, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation matches are Handlebars examples, Markdown code fences, rule names, or documentation text. The skill still has medium publish risk because it instructs the agent to run a bundled Python converter, reads and writes local files, and includes live Mailtrap API examples that use bearer tokens.

medium_findings.title: Bundled Converter Runs a Local Python Script
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to execute scripts/converter.py with user-selected provider, input, and output paths. The script reads template files, creates the output directory, and writes converted HTML plus report files, which is expected behavior but requires user-visible file boundaries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution path is explicit in SKILL.md and the converter shows concrete local file reads and writes. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Includes Live Mailtrap API Requests
medium_findings.description: The migration guide includes curl examples for creating, listing, updating, deleting, and sending Mailtrap templates with MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN. These examples are legitimate, but copied commands can modify remote Mailtrap resources or send email if used with real credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls and bearer token headers are directly visible in the guide. They target Mailtrap service endpoints for the documented migration workflow, not an unknown exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command findings come from Markdown code fences, inline code, and Handlebars template delimiters. They do not show Ruby backtick execution or shell interpolation in executable source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples and Handlebars syntax, not executable Ruby or shell code. The only real command execution is the intentional Python converter invocation recorded as medium risk.

low_findings.title: Crypto, C2, Reconnaissance, and Obfuscation Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity static matches map to template rule labels, provider names, sample IDs, and repeated Handlebars braces. No evidence found of command-and-control logic, cryptographic downgrade behavior, system reconnaissance, or obfuscated payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context shows conversion tables and regex replacement rules, not covert networking or malware behavior. The repeated bracket chains are expected for Handlebars templates.

low_findings.title: Secret Handling Is Advisory Rather Than Programmatic
low_findings.description: The guide mentions MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN, .env files, and secret managers while advising users not to hardcode credentials. No evidence found that the converter reads environment variables or extracts secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The secret references are documentation guidance and curl placeholders. The Python script uses command-line paths and does not access environment variables in the reviewed lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the agent to run the bundled converter script. This is required for deterministic conversion but should be visible to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and script entry point are explicit. The script performs conversion work and exits with documented status codes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read and Write Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The converter reads the selected input template and writes converted HTML, reports, and summaries to the selected output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are direct Path read_text, write_text, and mkdir calls. They are bounded by the user-provided input and output paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Live API Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide contains curl commands that call Mailtrap API endpoints with a bearer token placeholder.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint URLs and Authorization header are shown in the guide. They are legitimate Mailtrap examples, not hidden network calls.

content.value_statement: Migrating templates between email providers often breaks variables, loops, conditionals, and API field names. This skill converts common provider syntax to Mailtrap Handlebars and flags changes that need application logic.
actual_capabilities: Converts SendGrid insert helpers and flags unsupported SendGrid helper logic.
Converts Mandrill Mailchimp merge tags into lowercase Handlebars variables and conditionals.
Converts Postmark Mustachio conditionals and alternate unescaped HTML syntax.
Converts common Brevo Pongo2 variables, loops, conditionals, and default filters.
Processes one HTML file or a directory of HTML templates with per-file reports.
Explains Mailtrap API field changes for template UUIDs and template variables.
limitations: It does not create Mailtrap templates automatically unless the user runs the documented API steps.
It cannot safely convert date formatting, comparisons, and complex logic that must move into application code.
Brevo nested loops and ambiguous Postmark closing tags still need human review.
It only processes HTML template files in directory mode and does not scan nested folders.

use_cases.title: Move Transactional Templates to Mailtrap
use_cases.description: Convert welcome, password reset, receipt, and notification templates before switching providers.

use_cases.title: Audit Template Logic Before Migration
use_cases.description: Identify helper functions, date formatting, and conditionals that need application-side replacement.

use_cases.title: Prepare Bulk Template Libraries
use_cases.description: Convert many exported HTML templates into a separate output directory with conversion reports.
use_cases.target_user: Operations teams managing large email libraries

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this SendGrid HTML template file to Mailtrap Handlebars and place the result in a converted directory.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Provider Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert every Postmark HTML template in this folder to Mailtrap format and summarize files that need review.

prompt_templates.title: Review Manual Flags
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the conversion report and explain which flagged patterns need application code changes before we import the template.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Full Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these Brevo templates, classify every manual flag, and produce an API field mapping checklist for updating our send calls.

output_examples.input: A SendGrid order confirmation template with default values and comparison helpers.
output: Converted Handlebars template saved in the output directory.
Report listing automatic default value conversions.
Manual action note to replace comparison helpers with precomputed boolean variables.

output_examples.input: A folder of Postmark templates using Mustachio conditionals.
output: Bulk summary showing converted files, clean files, and templates needing review.
Per-template report describing conditional conversions and ambiguous closing tags.

output_examples.input: Inline Brevo HTML pasted into the chat.
output: Mailtrap-compatible Handlebars draft with unsupported syntax commented for review.
List of filters and nested loops that require application-side or manual changes.

best_practices: Run conversions on copied exports and keep the original provider templates unchanged.
Review every generated report before importing templates into Mailtrap.
Move formatting, comparisons, and business logic into application code before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API tokens into shared prompts, reports, or example commands.
Do not assume a clean conversion means rendered output matches the original provider.
Do not migrate mixed-provider folders without separating templates by source provider.

faq.question: Which providers can this skill convert from?
faq.answer: It supports SendGrid, Mailgun, Mandrill, Postmark, Brevo, and Amazon SES template migration to Mailtrap.

faq.question: Does it support bulk conversion?
faq.answer: Yes. It can process a directory of HTML files and write converted templates plus a summary report.

faq.question: Will it update my application send code?
faq.answer: No. It explains required API field changes, but code updates remain a separate task.

faq.question: Can it convert custom helper logic?
faq.answer: No. Date formatting, comparisons, and complex logic should be computed in application code.

faq.question: Does it send emails or create templates automatically?
faq.answer: No. The guide includes Mailtrap API examples, but the converter script only processes local template files.

faq.question: Is it safe for production templates?
faq.answer: Use copied exports, review all flags, test rendering in Mailtrap Email Testing, and avoid sharing real tokens.
